name: ASML Holding N.V.
window: Q2 2026 · print Jul 15 2026 (prior Q1 window Apr 15 → Jul 14 retained)

events.text: Q1 2026: net sales €8.8B (in guide); system sales €6.3B of which EUV >€4.1B (incl. 2 High-NA) and non-EUV >€2.1B; mix ~50/50 Logic 49%/Memory 51%. IBM €2.5B (above guide). GM 53.0% (top end). Net income €2.8B, margin 31.4%, EPS €7.15. FY26 raised/narrowed to €36-40B, GM 51-53%, H2-weighted. Low-NA EUV to at least 80 systems in 2027 (from 44 in 2025).

DRAM is the new leg — the market shifted materially vs CMD'24, EUV-layer adoption rising 1b (~3-4) → 1c (~6-7) → 1d (~10 by ~2028); BofA's base moves ASML to Top Pick Dec'25. ASP/GM ladder locked in: D→E (~55% GM)→F (high-50s/low-60s, immersion-like), wafer capacity >2x even with EUV units ~2x. FY26 guide-raise driven by immersion+IBM, 'nothing to do with EUV'.

AI demand structurally outpaces supply through 2027+; lithography is 'clearly the binding constraint'. Near-term risk to the reaction: the guide-raise was driven by immersion+IBM, which does not by itself signal incremental EUV strength.

High-NA primer: one High-NA exposure replaces low-NA multi-patterning, cutting steps from 'several dozen to sub-10' (~75% fewer) — a 'no-brainer' case. Mid-field stitching 'is not as serious as previously thought'. DRAM 'eminently suitable' (likely early-adopter). TSMC a deliberate slow-adopter, Intel pushing harder. Quoted throughputs (170/230/250 wph) are 'demonstration numbers'. Resist transition CAR→MOR below ~12-13nm.

'The Street is Behind the Curve': the message shifted from '≥80 units' to capacity — the 90-tool capacity 'is not the ceiling'; ASML can expand output without new clean rooms (cycle ~20→14 weeks by 2H27, free up R&D bays, use High-NA capacity for Low-NA, fast shipments). JPM thinks ASML can do 110 Low-NA EUV or more without new buildings. PT €1,900, FY27/28 rev €55.4B/€63.8B.

WFE to ~$250B in CY28, possibly in the $300s in CY29 (A-rated semicaps would gain ~$50/share). ASML ~110 EUV 'slots' of physical capacity ≈ ~$225B WFE before needing to add floor space. On DRAM-dollar leverage: ASML ~30% and AMAT ~25% of DRAM WFE dollars (>50% combined) — the two most levered to DRAM.

Raised to €1,900 (from €1,570) Buy, ASML 'remains our top pick among semicaps' post Silicon Valley bus tour — raised FY27-30 EPS +9-12%, sitting 14% ahead of consensus; $68bn lithography market and $54bn of ASML system sales in 2030 at a stable 91% share. At peer multiples ASML would be €2,170 vs PT €1,900.

ASML primer: Bernstein Outperform (May-21), P/E FY25A/26E/27E = 51.2x/38.7x/26.9x — multiple compression brought FY27E to 26.9x, 'directly aligned with AMAT' and below KLAC/LRCX; premium over SPE median compressed from 1.7x historical to ~1.1-1.2x. PT spread: Bernstein €1,700 / Kepler €1,460 / ING €1,700 / Santander Underperform €980. China normalizing to ~20% of 2026 sales.

Asia Trip Takeaways: TSMC already booking 40-45 EUV units for 2027 and finding it 'hard to get more' as memory makers compete for the same tools — read-through of EUV sold-out/capacity-allocated in 2027. But caution: buy-side expectations for ASML have already risen a lot ('a lot is already priced in') after the narrative turned to 'we can do at least 100 EUV machines'.

'The Catalyst Path Ahead' — OW/Top Pick, PT raised to €1,830 (from €1,660). Lifts FY26/27 EUV sales (incl High-NA) to 75/92 (from 69/90) on a more-material SK Hynix (6 tools vs 3). Frames ASML a summer 'catch-up trade' (+75% YTD but -34% vs US semicap) on 3 debates — capacity growth, FY28 WFE, pricing power — into the 07-15 print; broadly in-line quarter expected with a possible guidance uptick on robust DRAM demand + memory LTAs. MSe FY27e EPS €47.38 (+7% vs cons).

Q2 2026 beat & raise: net sales €9.33B (+6.4% q/q, +21.3% y/y, above €8.4-9.0B guide high end); GM 54.0% (vs 51-52% guide / street 52.0%); OpM 37.1%; net income €2.92B; diluted EPS €7.58 (~11% above street €6.85, vs €7.15 in Q1). System sales €6.56B (16 EUV = €3.74B, 1 High-NA), IBM €2.76-2.8B (3800E PEP-E lifts throughput 220→230 wph). Korea 43% of system sales (2nd-highest ever), Memory 49% of revenue, China just 14% (lowest since Q1'23). Q3'26 guide rev €11-12B (mid €11.5B, +23% q/q / +53% y/y vs cons €10.3B), GM 55-57%. FY26 guide RAISED to €43-45B at 54-56% GM — 2nd raise this year, >10ppt top-line upgrade with six months left.

The headline: Low-NA EUV & ArF-immersion capacity to rise +30% each in BOTH 2027 and 2028 (customer-demand-backed, orders extending into 2028 with significant down payments) → EUV cap ~85 ('27) / ~110 ('28) + ~20 High-NA for '27/'28; ArFi 130 ('26) → 170 ('27) → 220 ('28). The 85 EUV for 2027 came slightly BELOW the buy-side 90-100 hope (the one soft spot) but mgmt stressed numbers 'can still be raised'. Milestone: Intel now running High-NA in production on 18A; ASML rev-rec'd ~5 High-NA tools in 2026.

Post-print: sees a 20-30% lift to '27/'28 consensus EPS despite 2027 EUV of only 85 — driven by DUV immersion +30% in BOTH '27 & '28 (vs cons +11%/+6%, the big upside, tightly correlated with EUV), GM upside (high-50s, >60% for new models, sustainable) and emerging value/overlay-based pricing power (new for '27/'28 orders; EUV+immersion ≈70% of revenue). FY26 top-line upgraded >10ppt → implied Q4 annualized rev ~$56B, >10% above Street's 2027; memory rev +75% y/y (vs ~40-50% WFE-memory). High-NA orders on track H2'26/early'27 from 'all customers' incl. memory.

'Triple happiness' — Outperform, PT raised €2,300 → €2,500 (new Street-high). Solid beat & raise on top line, capacity and margins with further upside on pricing/capacity: (1) +30% Low-NA EUV & ArFi capacity each year '27/'28 (EUV cap 85/110; DUV 220 in '28 'certainly above expectations'); (2) pricing uplift from productivity 'often overlooked'; (3) gross-margin upside.

'Visibility extending, margins expanding' — Buy, PT raised €1,900 → €2,100 (Top Pick in semicaps). FY26E-30E EPS +10-26% on higher ArF(i) volumes + GM (raised 300-350bps/yr FY26-28): net sales FY26/27/28 €44.1/55.8/69.2B (+11%/+12%/+22% vs cons), EPS(dil) $40.2/56.8/77.5, GM 55.7%/57.6%/59.4%, FY28E FCF €30.5B; 43% EBIT CAGR FY25-28E. Smoothed EUV units to 90 ('27, from 100) / 110 ('28, from 102); ArF(i) rev FY27-28 +24%/35%. ASML now ~8% discount to semicap peers vs 40-60% historical premium.

cells: order intake 'very strong'; no number given
order intake strong; High-NA EUV orders expected H2'26/early-'27 from 'all customers' incl. memory
300k+ High-NA wafers; SK hynix EXE:5200 in fab
Intel accepted EXE:5200B for HVM; 48 EUV sold
>500k wafers, >80% avail.; Low-NA to ≥80 units in '27
Low-NA EUV & ArFi capacity +30%/yr for '27 & '28 → EUV cap ~85 ('27)/~110 ('28); DUV imm. cap ~220 ('28); Intel running High-NA in production on 18A
China just 14% of system sales (lowest since Q1'23); CFO keeps FY26 China at ~20% → implies sharp 2H reaccel
Memory HBM/DDR 'very strong,' tight thru '26; Logic 4→3nm
Memory rev +75% y/y (vs ~40-50% WFE-memory); litho-intensity rising 1c→1d; demand strong across Logic+Memory
FY26 raised again €43-45B, GM 54-56% (2nd raise this yr, >10ppt top-line)
AI investment driving Logic+Memory; pricing power emerging (value/overlay-based, not just throughput)
